title: hieracium porrifolium subsp. longibracteum
intro: Hieracium porrifolium subsp. longibracteum, also known as hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The health benefits of Hieracium porrifolium subsp. longibracteum include its ability to support the immune system, reduce fever, and act as a diuretic, making it useful in treating conditions such as urinary tract infections. In herbal preparations, Hieracium porrifolium subsp. longibracteum is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is also used topically in creams and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
